title: senecio cheesemanii
intro: Senecio cheesemanii, also known as the Cheeseman's ragwort, is a plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant's leaves and stems contain toxic compounds, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an expectorant, making it a natural remedy for coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, Senecio cheesemanii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to exercise caution and follow proper guidelines when using this plant due to its toxicity.
